items.label: About ZYNQ.peer
items.content:
# ZYNQ.peer

Lightweight WebRTC wrapper built on PeerJS. Designed for simple and fast peer-to-peer communication (messaging, audio, video).

### Features

- Peer-to-peer messaging (1-to-1 + broadcast)
- Audio & video calls
- Built-in invite / accept flow
- Heartbeat + timeout system
- Automatic stream management (reuse + cleanup)
- Bitrate monitoring (video only)
- Event-driven API
- No backend required

items.label: Quick Start
items.content:
# Quick Start

### Setup
```javascript
import 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@0.2.5/index.min.js'
await ZYNQ.load("peer")
await ZYNQ.peer.start()
```

### Get your ID
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('ready', id => {
  console.log('My ID:', id)
})
```

### Connect
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.connect('peer-id')
```

### Send message
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.sendTo('peer-id', 'Hello')
```

### Receive messages
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('message', ({ peerId, message }) => {
  console.log(peerId, message)
})
```

items.label: Start
items.content:
# Core API

### Start peer
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.start(customId?)
```
- Optional custom ID
- Connects to broker
- Emits `ready`

items.label: Destroy
items.content:
### Destroy
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.destroy()
```
- Closes all connections
- Stops media devices
- Clears state
- Emits `destroyed`

items.label: Events
items.content:
# Events

### Listen
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('event', callback)
```

### Event list

| Event | Data | Description |
|------|------|------------|
| `ready` | id | Your peer ID |
| `invite` | {id, config, accept, reject} | Incoming connection |
| `joined` | peerId | Connected |
| `left` | peerId | Disconnected |
| `message` | {peerId, message} | Chat message |
| `local-stream` | MediaStream | Local media |
| `remote-stream` | {peerId, stream} | Remote media |
| `bitrate` | {peerId, kbps} | Connection quality |
| `rejected` | peerId | Call rejected |
| `error` | {code, peerId?, message} | Error info |
| `destroyed` | - | Cleanup |

### Notes
- Use `accept()` / `reject()` on `invite`
- `bitrate` fires ~every 2s (video only)

items.label: Messaging
items.content:
# Messaging

### Send to one peer
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.sendTo(peerId, message)
```

### Broadcast
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.broadcast(message)
```

### Receive
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('message', ({ peerId, message }) => {})
```

Supports any data type (string, object, array, etc.)

items.label: Audio & Video
items.content:
# Media

### Connect with media
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.connect('peer-id', {
  video: true,
  audio: true,
  resolution: 720,
  fps: 30
})
```

### Handle streams
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('local-stream', stream => {
  localVideo.srcObject = stream
})

ZYNQ.peer.on('remote-stream', ({ stream }) => {
  remoteVideo.srcObject = stream
})
```

- Streams are automatically managed
- Camera stops when no peers remain

items.label: Connections
items.content:
# Connections

### Disconnect peer
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.disconnect(peerId)
```

### Get your ID
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.getMyId()
```

### Active peers
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.getActivePeers()
```
Returns an array of peer IDs

items.label: System & Advanced
items.content:
# System

### Behavior
- Heartbeat: every 2s
- Timeout: 12s inactivity
- Stream reuse + auto cleanup
- Bitrate only when video is active

### Debug
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setDebug(2)
```
0 = off  
1 = normal  
2+ = verbose

### Disable auto-start
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setAutoStart(false)
```
